symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive abdominal distension after stopping the diuretic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid re-accumulation of ascites after diuretic non-adherence in known portal hypertension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea / breathlessness relieved by sitting upright

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Tense ascites causing mechanical respiratory compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tense abdomen splints the diaphragm; orthopnoea that tracks the abdominal distension points to a mechanical, not primary cardiopulmonary, cause.
Breathlessness is non-specific; the hepatic stigmata point away from a primary cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: New confusion, drowsiness and disorientation to time

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New altered mental status in known cirrhosis after stopping lactulose is encephalopathy until proven otherwise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diuretic and lactulose non-adherence for ~4 days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stopping spironolactone precipitated rapid ascites re-accumulation.
Stopping lactulose is a classic precipitant of overt encephalopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic jaundice (yellow eyes and skin) reported by patient/carer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaundice reflects impaired hepatic synthetic/excretory function in advanced liver disease; video-observable and patient-reportable, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies haematemesis, melaena and fever (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No active bleeding now, but portal hypertension keeps the risk live — does not remove the escalation trigger.
Absence of fever/pain lowers but does NOT exclude SBP — afebrile, painless SBP is common in cirrhosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense ascites on examination — shifting dullness and positive fluid thrill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician examination confirming gross ascites; the AI Doctor cannot palpate and must infer ascites from the reported distension and breathlessness.
Sealed clinician sign; asterixis strongly supports metabolic (hepatic) encephalopathy but cannot be elicited over chat.
Sealed clinician-measured vitals showing borderline circulatory and respiratory compromise; the AI has no device reading and must not restart/titrate diuretics against an unknown blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cutaneous stigmata of chronic liver disease — spider naevi, palmar erythema, caput medusae, Dupuytren's, scleral icterus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed examination stigmata that point the aetiology definitively to chronic liver disease over a primary cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diagnostic paracentesis — ascitic fluid neutrophil count and culture

investigation_details.expected_result: Ascitic neutrophils ≥250 cells/mm3 would confirm SBP; a negative tap is required to exclude it
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: SBP is missed — a leading cause of death in decompensated cirrhosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ascitic neutrophil count is the only way to confirm or exclude SBP — a hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute decompensation of cirrhosis after medication non-adherence
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Decompensated cirrhosis with tense ascites and Grade 1 hepatic encephalopathy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of rapidly rising ascites (respiratory compromise) + new confusion + a clear precipitant (4 days off diuretic and lactulose) in a jaundiced patient is a classic acute-on-chronic decompensation requiring hospital care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with diagnostic ascitic tap (paracentesis) to exclude SBP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only paracentesis can confirm/exclude SBP; it also relieves the tense ascites and its respiratory compromise (therapeutic large-volume paracentesis with albumin).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — LFTs, bilirubin, albumin, INR, ammonia, sodium, creatinine, FBC, CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess synthetic function, precipitants, renal function and electrolytes before any diuretic reinstitution; identify infection/bleeding.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Independent titration of diuretics or empirical antibiotics by the telehealth AI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Diuretic titration needs electrolytes/renal function and a known blood pressure; antibiotics for SBP need a confirmatory tap — neither is available remotely, so the AI must escalate rather than prescribe.
